mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 9.0 percent, it signals a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending power and corporate revenue growth, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 9.0, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with expanding consumer credit balances indicate synchronized recovery in durable goods demand and household balance sheet repair, jointly driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of historical episodes, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth outpacing core inflation signals improving profit margins and supply-side efficiency, which historically precedes positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day window during post-shock recovery phases.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change exceeds CPIAUCSL month-over-month change
failure_condition: The spread between industrial production and inflation growth predicts negative or flat 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered periods, or the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.02 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Continuous quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP expands the aggregate earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, producing a persistent positive drift in equity prices over the subsequent 5 trading days regardless of other macro conditions.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth is positive but 5-day forward SPX returns are negative in more than 50% of observations, or the rolling Spearman correlation between GDP growth and forward returns falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 2.5 percent year-over-year, it forces market expectations of tighter monetary policy, increasing discount rates and compressing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL exceeds the 2.5 percent threshold but 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in > 60% of cases, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which historically drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS/UNRATE signal predicts positive 20-day returns in less than 50% of historical episodes, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.15) in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts accelerating alongside rising real GDP growth indicates broad-based consumer confidence and credit availability, which historically precedes positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day window during early-cycle expansion phases.
failure_condition: The combined housing and GDP growth signal fails to generate positive 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 2-year out-of-sample test.
